description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a metal framework. The solar panels are arranged in a grid pattern, elevated above a patterned base that simulates a paved or concrete surface. The supporting structure consists of angled metal posts and cross-bracing, providing stability and elevating the panels to optimize sunlight exposure. The panels themselves have a dark, glassy appearance with a visible grid of cells, typical of crystalline silicon solar modules. The base beneath the structure is visually segmented with geometric patterns, suggesting an urban or landscaped setting. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present, and the structure appears fixed and robust, suitable for outdoor urban environments.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a metal support frame, elevated above a patterned urban base. The grid of dark photovoltaic panels is supported by angled metal posts.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the solar array from an elevated angle, highlighting the grid of panels and the supporting metal framework beneath, as well as the patterned base.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a side-on perspective, emphasizing the height of the structure and the angled support posts, with the rectangular panel array clearly visible.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a rear view, showing the back of the solar panels and the full extent of the supporting framework and patterned ground.
description_view_3: View 3 displays the opposite side, again focusing on the support structure and the grid arrangement of the panels from a different angle.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum posts and cross-bracing
attribute_array_configuration: single rectangular grid of approximately 12 panels arranged in 3 rows by 4 columns
